---
title: "Path to Mathematical Research"
subtitle: "The path that asks not just 'how does it work?' but 'why is it true?'"
---

## What you can do at the end of this path

- Read mathematics papers in your chosen subfield with the proofs as the main
  attraction, not as obstacles.
- Construct your own proofs of non-trivial results, and recognise when an
  argument is or isn't valid.
- Move comfortably between the analytic, algebraic, and topological views of
  the same object.

## The path

Research mathematics is the long path: every Tier matters, but the proof-based
mindset begins to dominate from Tier 5 onward.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[Tier 1<br/>Number sense, primes, variables] --> B[Tier 2-3<br/>Algebra & geometry done carefully]
    B --> C[Tier 4<br/>Calculus]
    C --> D[Tier 5<br/>Real analysis, linear algebra, intro number theory]
    D --> E[Tier 6<br/>Abstract algebra, complex analysis, topology intro]
    E --> F[Tier 7 — Research track:<br/>Measure theory<br/>Functional analysis<br/>Topology<br/>your speciality]

    classDef built fill:#c8e6c9,stroke:#2e7d32
    class A built
```

## Where to start right now

The research path begins with **What is a Number?** in Tier 1 — the very
question itself, not glossed over but examined. Then **Variables — Names for
Unknown Numbers** introduces the symbolic move.

## What's built / what's planned

- ✅ Tier 1: *What is a Number?* (exemplar lesson)
- 📋 Everything beyond — planned, not yet authored.
